seed_games.title: Balatro

source_refs.title: Balatro store page
source_refs.notes: Primary reference for the game candidate.

seed_games.short_description: Poker-inspired roguelike deckbuilder built around familiar card semantics.
seed_games.selection_reason: Strong sample for transforming familiar rules into systemic depth.

seed_games.title: Into the Breach

source_refs.title: Into the Breach store page
source_refs.notes: Primary reference for the game candidate.

seed_games.short_description: Compact tactical game using small boards, complete information, and readable enemy intent.
seed_games.selection_reason: Strong sample for high tactical depth with constrained presentation scope.

seed_games.title: Baba Is You

source_refs.title: Baba Is You store page
source_refs.notes: Primary reference for the game candidate.

seed_games.short_description: Puzzle game where players manipulate explicit rules as objects.
seed_games.selection_reason: Strong sample for rule manipulation as a central design pattern.

design_claims.subject: Balatro
design_claims.object: familiar card rules to reduce learning cost
design_claims.explanation: Poker-like hands and card values give players an immediate rules vocabulary before modifiers add depth.

evidence.title: Balatro design summary
evidence.quote_or_summary: The game starts from recognizable poker hand logic and layers score modifiers over it.
evidence.notes: Curated design interpretation for the fixture.

design_claims.subject: Into the Breach
design_claims.object: high tactical depth through compact boards and visible enemy intent
design_claims.explanation: Small board size and readable future actions let players reason about consequences without large production scope.

evidence.title: Into the Breach design summary
evidence.quote_or_summary: Encounters emphasize small grids, visible threats, and consequence planning.
evidence.notes: Curated design interpretation for the fixture.

design_claims.subject: Baba Is You
design_claims.explanation: Rules become explicit objects that players can inspect and alter.

evidence.title: Baba Is You design summary
evidence.quote_or_summary: Puzzle rules are represented as manipulable language-like objects.
evidence.notes: Curated design interpretation for the fixture.

design_claims.subject: symbolic tactical UI
design_claims.explanation: Abstract symbolic feedback can reduce art load, but this depends on clarity and feedback design.

evidence.title: Curator hypothesis
evidence.quote_or_summary: Symbolic UI is expected to be cheaper than animation-heavy combat for a solo prototype.
evidence.notes: Weak evidence included to prove downstream confidence handling.

constraints.statement: Prefer concepts that can start with symbolic or low-volume art.

related_innovation_patterns: combine rule editing with short tactical runs
recommended_transformations: compress tactical space into a small board
replace animation-heavy feedback with symbolic state changes
combine editable rules with short encounter runs
opportunity_frame.fit_reason: The area emphasizes systemic depth and readable rules over asset volume, matching a strong-programming solo developer with weak art capacity.
opportunity_frame.risk_reason: The largest risk is whether players can understand rule consequences quickly enough for short tactical runs.

concept_cards.title: Ruleforge Tactics
concept_cards.one_sentence_concept: A short-run tactics game where players win by editing small battlefield rules instead of directly commanding every unit.
concept_cards.core_fantasy: Outsmarting a compact battlefield by rewriting its laws.
concept_cards.core_loop: Inspect enemy intents, draft rule cards, apply one rule change, resolve the turn, and adapt the next encounter.
main_player_decisions: when to accept a risky rule interaction
which encounter reward supports the current rule plan
concept_cards.difference_from_references: It uses tactical forecasting and rule manipulation, but turns them into short encounter runs rather than poker scoring, pure tactics, or puzzle levels.
concept_cards.fit_reason: The concept can begin with symbols, simple boards, and rules-first feedback before any polished asset work.
production_risks: symbolic presentation may still require careful UI polish
design_risks: players may not predict rule consequences
runs may feel arbitrary if rule feedback is unclear
concept_cards.novelty_reason: It combines rule editing with tactical run structure under low-art production constraints.
concept_cards.suggested_prototype_scope: A 4x4 board, three unit types, six rule cards, and five encounters.

prototype_brief.largest_risk_hypothesis: Players can understand and enjoy changing battlefield rules within the first few minutes.
prototype_brief.minimum_prototype_scope: A 4x4 board, three unit types, six rule cards, and five encounters.
success_signals: player can explain a rule consequence after one encounter
player voluntarily restarts to try a different rule combination
failure_signals: player cannot predict what a rule change will affect
